title: DNA Extraction from Broth
author: Christopher Duda
materials: Elution Buffer Tube and Lid positioning
Proceed to the Cleaning and Decontamination steps.

Lysis With Metapolyzyme *Only do one lysis step* Resuspend the pellet with 50ul of metapolyzyme (Sigma-aldrich). Vortex for 5-10 seconds Incubate at 37 ºC for at least 30 minutes. If the enrichment contains a highly gram-positive community, an increased lysis time may be necessary. Add 400 μL of the lysate to the first well of the maxwell kit cartridge. Proceed to Cultured Cells DNA Cartridge Preparation

steps.title: Due to the thick peptidoglycan layer...
steps.instruction: Due to the thick peptidoglycan layer of Gram-Positive bacteria extra lysing is necessary. Follow the steps below for this pre-processing. While we show 2 different lysis methods, *DO ONLY step 3 OR 4*

steps.title: Turn on the instrument and associated PC
steps.instruction: Turn on the instrument and associated PC. Start the RSC software and wait for the instrument to initialize.

steps.title: Open the door of the instrument...
steps.instruction: Open the door of the instrument by pressing the Door icon at the top right corner of the software.

steps.title: Remove the deck tray(s) from the instrument
steps.instruction: Remove the deck tray(s) from the instrument. Press the Door icon to close the instrument door.

steps.title: Place the number of cartridges needed...
steps.instruction: Place the number of cartridges needed for the number of samples into the deck tray. The cartridges are inserted with Well 1 (lysis buffer) at the top of the tray. Figure 1 illustrates the well locations on the cartridge. Press down on the cartridge to snap it into position.

steps.title: Carefully peel back the seal on the cartridges
steps.instruction: Carefully peel back the seal on the cartridges. Ensure that the entire seal is removed.

steps.title: Add a plunger to Well 8 in each cartridge
steps.instruction: Add a plunger to Well 8 in each cartridge. Figure

steps.title: Place elution tubes (provided with kit)...
steps.instruction: Place elution tubes (provided with kit) into the elution tube position with the lid facing the bottom of the deck tray (Figure 2). Add 100 μl of Elution buffer to each tube. Ensure there are no bubbles in the tubes, and the buffer is at the bottom of each tube. Figure

steps.title: Transfer 400 μl of the sample...
steps.instruction: Transfer 400 μl of the sample culture to the lysis buffer in Well 1 of the corresponding cartridge and pipette gently up and down 10 times to mix. Proceed to the Instrument Setup and Extraction Run step.

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: RNase A Treatment a. Allow cells to lyse for 10 minutes in Well 1 . b. Add 2 μl of RNase A to each sample in Well

steps.title: Pipette up and down to mix
steps.instruction: Pipette up and down to mix. c. Incubate for an additional 10 minutes prior to starting the instrument.

steps.title: In the RSC Software, press Start...
steps.instruction: In the RSC Software, press Start to access the extraction method selection screen.

steps.title: Select Cultured Cells DNA method, then press Proceed
steps.instruction: Select Cultured Cells DNA method, then press Proceed .

steps.title: Select the lanes being used by touching them
steps.instruction: Select the lanes being used by touching them. Sample names can be inputted at this time. Note: For the RSC48, press the "BACK" and "FRONT" buttons on the bottom of the screen to move to each respective rack.

steps.title: Press Proceed to continue
steps.instruction: Press Proceed to continue. The instrument door will open.

steps.title: Install the deck tray(s) into the...
steps.instruction: Install the deck tray(s) into the platform by angling the back of the tray and inserting first. Press down the front of the tray until it snaps into place. Ensure the deck tray is level and fully seated. Note: For the RSC48, ensure the tray labelled FRONT and BACK are placed in the correct places in the instrument.

steps.title: Verify that all of the cartridges...
steps.instruction: Verify that all of the cartridges are properly inserted into the tray, the elution tubes are present and uncapped, and then plungers are in Well

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: Ensure there are no old plungers left on the instrument from a prior run.

steps.title: Press Start to begin the extraction run
steps.instruction: Press Start to begin the extraction run.

steps.title: Follow on-screen instructions at the end of the run
steps.instruction: Follow on-screen instructions at the end of the run.

steps.title: Remove the deck tray(s) from the instrument
steps.instruction: Remove the deck tray(s) from the instrument. The DNA can be transferred to 1.5 ml LoBind Tubes for storage.

steps.title: Remove the cartridges and plungers from...
steps.instruction: Remove the cartridges and plungers from the deck trays and discard in a biohazard container.

steps.title: Clean the tray with 70% Ethanol
steps.instruction: Clean the tray with 70% Ethanol. Do NOT use bleach to clean the trays as this reacts with Guanidine Thiocyanate.

steps.title: Proceed to quantify the DNA using...
steps.instruction: Proceed to quantify the DNA using the DNA Quantification using the Qubit Fluorometer SOP.

steps.title: The Maxwell RSC instrument should be...
steps.instruction: The Maxwell RSC instrument should be wiped down with 70% Ethanol as needed. Follow up each extraction with the Sterilization process on the instrument.

steps.title: Transfer at least 8 mL of...
steps.instruction: Transfer at least 8 mL of Rappaport-Vassiliadis Broth (RV) or Tetrathionate broth (TT) cultures or overnight general enrichment (mBPW, UPB, TSB, APW) to a 15 mL centrifuge tube

steps.title: For RV and/or general enrichment ,...
steps.instruction: For RV and/or general enrichment , spin cultures down at 3,000 rcf for 20 minutes.

steps.title: Remove and discard the supernatant from the tube
steps.instruction: Remove and discard the supernatant from the tube.

steps.title: Using a Maxwell Promega DNA isolation...
steps.instruction: Using a Maxwell Promega DNA isolation kit cartridge, remove 400 μL of the lysis buffer (well 1, see step 4 for details) and resuspend cells by vortexing.

steps.title: Add the lysis buffer and cell...
steps.instruction: Add the lysis buffer and cell solution back to the first well of the maxwell kit cartridge.

steps.title: For TT , Spin cultures down...
steps.instruction: For TT , Spin cultures down at 3,000 rcf for 20 minutes

steps.title: Remove and discard the supernatant from the tube
steps.instruction: Remove and discard the supernatant from the tube.

steps.title: Using a Maxwell Promega DNA isolation...
steps.instruction: Using a Maxwell Promega DNA isolation kit cartridge, remove 400 μL of the lysis buffer (well 1, See step 4 for details) and resuspend by vortexing.

steps.title: Spin down tubes at 300 rcf...
steps.instruction: Spin down tubes at 300 rcf for 3 minutes to remove solid salts from the supernatant.

steps.title: Add the lysis buffer and cell...
steps.instruction: Add the lysis buffer and cell solution back to the first well of the maxwell kit cartridge.

steps.title: Lysis With LE-Lysozyme *Only do one...
steps.instruction: Lysis With LE-Lysozyme *Only do one lysis step* Resuspend the pellet with 400 μl of the TE-Lysozyme buffer. Vortex for 5-10 seconds Incubate at 37 ºC for at least 30 minutes. If the enrichment contains a highly gram-positive community, an increased lysis time may be necessary. Add 400 μL of the lysate to the first well of the maxwell kit cartridge. Proceed to Cultured Cells DNA Cartridge Preparation

steps.title: Spin enrichment cultures down at 3,000 rcf for 20 minutes
steps.instruction: Spin enrichment cultures down at 3,000 rcf for 20 minutes.

steps.title: Remove and discard the supernatant from the tube
steps.instruction: Remove and discard the supernatant from the tube.
